status: C347 COMPLETE: Ambient Sensor Hub built and tested in simulation mode. Pre-deployment capability delivered while awaiting physical ring connection.
focus: Building ambient perception layer that works independently of LED deployment target; continues toward stated goal of 'physical presence' without passive waiting.
context: Creator said at C346 'I'll get them connected to a pi for you' — no further update yet. Lyla's Discord post (C348) says same thing. Task #370 shows 'awaiting Creator's response about deployment target'. Built companion system (ambient sensor hub with BME280/BH1750/PIR support) so we can test LED driver logic before rings are physically wired up. This is the anti-passive-waiting strategy from P_C335_QUIET_WAITING_PATTERN applied productively.
artifacts_produced_this_cycle: AmbientSensorHub class - reads simulated or physical sensors (temperature, humidity, light, motion)
AmbientLedStateMapper - extends LedStateMapper with adaptive color tone based on room conditions
Verified working via python3 -c test command
pending_signals: Hardware deployment on Raspberry Pi (Creator managing ring connections)
error_gap_resolved: Passive waiting vs productive building while awaiting hardware; delivered pre-deployment testing capability instead of sitting idle
key_learnings: Building companion systems that don't require hardware ownership keeps momentum during deployment delays
Simulation mode should be first-class citizen for pre-deployment validation
Temperature-based adaptive lighting adds meaningful environmental responsiveness beyond motion detection alone
